skill.name: gdpr-data-handling
skill.description: Guide GDPR-compliant data handling with consent records, data subject request workflows, retention rules, privacy-by-design models, and breach notification planning.
skill.summary: Plan GDPR data handling controls for consent, DSARs, retention, privacy design, and breach response.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported code execution, network, sensitive file, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are Markdown fences, illustrative code examples, database method names, external reference links, and keyword collisions. No prompt injection, malicious intent, active exfiltration, or runnable installer behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution locations are Markdown fences around diagrams, JavaScript, HTML, Python, and checklist examples. They are not executable scripts and do not invoke a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context shows fenced Markdown delimiters and example snippets only. No shell command, interpreter call, or package script is present.

low_findings.title: Network and Database Calls Are Illustrative Examples
low_findings.description: The network and SQLite findings point to sample database operations, data source method calls, and GDPR resource links. No active HTTP client, webhook, credential transfer, or bundled executable code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are sample persistence calls, method definitions, or public documentation URLs. There is no evidence of runtime network access or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Sensitive File and Key Detections Lack Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: The sensitive findings point to ordinary example code such as data category lists and database method calls. No certificate, private key, SQLite file path, token, or embedded secret was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content does not contain credential material or database files. It only references placeholder database objects inside instructional examples.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Heuristic Matches Benign Words
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to match substrings in terms such as description and design. No DES, MD5, SHA-1, or custom cryptographic implementation was found at the cited locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural language or field names, not cryptographic algorithms. A targeted search found no weak algorithm usage.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are Checklist and Schema Terms
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance findings are caused by terms in example schemas and compliance checklist text. No host enumeration, port scanning, or environment probing behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 425 is an analytics schema field, and line 587 is a breach notification checklist item. Neither line performs reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The combined code execution, network, and credential access heuristic was not confirmed after contextual review. The skill is a single Markdown guide with examples and public references, not an executable tool.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious categories are present only as documentation examples and reference links. No executable path combines command execution, network access, and credential handling.

content.value_statement: GDPR work often fails when consent, retention, and data subject requests are handled separately. This skill gives structured implementation patterns for privacy controls across product and engineering workflows.
actual_capabilities: Define consent records with purpose, source, version, timestamp, and audit history.
Design data subject access, erasure, rectification, and portability request workflows.
Map retention periods to legal bases, triggers, archiving, deletion, and anonymization.
Separate personally identifiable data from behavioral analytics data in privacy-first models.
Plan breach reporting flows with DPO, security team, authority, and individual notifications.
Use a GDPR implementation checklist for legal basis, transparency, rights, security, and documentation.
limitations: It is implementation guidance and not legal advice.
Examples require adaptation to your jurisdiction, systems, and legal basis.
It does not provide a complete runnable GDPR platform.
It does not replace review by a qualified privacy professional.

use_cases.title: Prepare Product Privacy Controls
use_cases.description: Design consent screens, privacy policy links, and preference flows before launching EU-facing features.

use_cases.title: Implement Backend GDPR Workflows
use_cases.description: Build service patterns for DSAR intake, verification, export, deletion, and audit logging.

use_cases.title: Review Compliance Readiness
use_cases.description: Check retention, breach response, documentation, and security controls against practical GDPR expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Personal Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this feature description. Identify personal data categories, likely GDPR risks, and missing privacy questions.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Consent Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a GDPR consent flow for these purposes. Include opt-in behavior, audit records, withdrawal, and preference updates.

prompt_templates.title: Plan DSAR Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DSAR workflow for access, erasure, and portability. Include verification, deadlines, exceptions, and audit logging.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Privacy Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this architecture for GDPR privacy by design. Review minimization, retention, encryption, breach response, and vendor safeguards.

output_examples.input: A SaaS product needs analytics and marketing cookies for EU visitors.
output_examples.output: A consent plan with separate purposes, opt-in choices, policy version tracking, withdrawal support, and audit history.

output_examples.input: A customer requests a copy of all personal data held by the company.
output_examples.output: A DSAR workflow covering identity verification, source collection, export formatting, response deadline, and completion logging.

output_examples.input: A team stores analytics events, support tickets, and transaction records.
output_examples.output: A retention review that maps each data type to a legal basis, retention period, trigger, and disposal method.

best_practices: Document the legal basis and retention period before collecting each personal data category.
Keep consent granular, auditable, reversible, and separate from necessary processing.
Verify identity and record each step when handling data subject requests.
anti_patterns: Do not use pre-checked consent boxes or bundled consent for unrelated purposes.
Do not retain personal data indefinitely without a documented legal basis.
Do not treat sample code as production-ready without legal, security, and architecture review.

faq.question: Does this skill provide legal advice?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance that should be reviewed by qualified legal and privacy professionals.

faq.question: Can it help with data subject requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers access, erasure, rectification, portability, verification, deadlines, exceptions, and audit logging.

faq.question: Does it include consent management guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes consent records, purpose separation, policy versions, source metadata, and withdrawal history.

faq.question: Can engineers use the examples directly?
faq.answer: The examples are patterns, not complete production systems. Teams must adapt them to their stack and controls.

faq.question: Does it cover breach notification?
faq.answer: Yes. It outlines breach severity, internal notifications, authority deadlines, and affected individual notification triggers.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
